Patch Set 4:

Build Started http://build.whamcloud.com/job/lustre-reviews/11154/

Patch Set 4: Looks good to me, but someone else must approve

There was some internal issue with the previous patch.  I have resubmitted the patch to resolve it.

Patch Set 4: (5 inline comments)

I'm not sure I understand the motivation or reasoning behind this change.  The pid+export is unique per mountpoint.  While there may be multiple mountpoints on a single client (NID) these are typically treated as separate clients from the Lustre point of view.

Can you please include in the commit comment under what circumstances this problem might be hit?  Is this purely a defect hit during testing with multiple mountpoints on a single client?
Patch Set 4: I would prefer that you didn't submit this

Patch Set 4: Verified

Build Successful 
 
http://build.whamcloud.com/job/lustre-reviews/11154/ : SUCCESS
Uploaded patch set 5.

Patch Set 5:

Build Started http://build.whamcloud.com/job/lustre-reviews/11155/

Patch Set 4: (5 inline comments)

This patch helps user to detect deadlocks. We can't rely on kernel locks because lustre lock is taken before kernel one. That is why it is needed to check all files from different lustre mountpoints.

Patch Set 5: Looks good to me, but someone else must approve

Your response still does not explain _how_ this could be a problem in real life?  Is this seen only during testing?  I can only imagine so, because that would be the only time where there isn't exactly one export per NID on the server.

Ah, looking at the LU-1601 ticket this is much more clear.  It would be more useful to have as clear a description in the patch commit comment as there is in the jira ticket.

I agree for this case that the NID+PID is the right identifier rather than export+PID.

There is some potential for performance impact, since there is an extra NID lookup for every call, but hopefully not significant.

Patch Set 5: Verified

Build Successful 
 
http://build.whamcloud.com/job/lustre-reviews/11155/ : SUCCESS
Uploaded patch set 6.

Patch Set 6:

I don't see what is different.  In looking at it, "unknown" has a second 'n' at the end.  Lastly, why all the extra "goto"s when an "else" works just fine?  'goto's are not evil, but they really ought to be avoided.

The error from the test is:
== sanityn test 61: flock deadlock: different mounts ================ 03:36:01 (1357817761)
1+0 records in
1+0 records out
1024 bytes (1.0 kB) copied, 0.00215944 s, 474 kB/s
1+0 records in
1+0 records out
1024 bytes (1.0 kB) copied, 0.00176509 s, 580 kB/s
13645: get lock2
13646: get lock1
13645: done
13645: get lock1
13646: done
13646: get lock2

It seems it never gets lock2?  This looks very related to your changes.   Can you have a good look at it please.

Patch Set 7:

The test can fail due to LU-1602
Lets land it first.

Patch Set 7:

Can you update your commit message and resubmit the patch for LU-1602?

parents.subject: LU-3808 ptlrpc: Return a meaningful status from ptlrpcd_init()

commit.subject: LU-1601 ldlm: Fix flock detection for different mounts
commit.message:
LU-1601 ldlm: Fix flock detection for different mounts

Deadlock can happen when 2 processes take concurrent locks
on files situated in different mountpoints.

Modify flock detection algorithm to distinguish process by
pair PID+NID instead of PID+export.
It is done by searching for a blocking owner in all OBD's
exports with the same NID.

Xyratex-bug-id: MRP-449
Signed-off-by: Andriy Skulysh <Andriy_Skulysh@xyratex.com>
Reviewed-by: Vitaly Fertman <vitaly_fertman@xyratex.com>
Reviewed-by: Bruce Korb <bruce_korb@xyratex.com>
Change-Id: Ib2969df9b9733af4025e1905caf2378af72c6f18
Reviewed-on: http://review.whamcloud.com/3276
Reviewed-by: Keith Mannthey <keith.mannthey@intel.com>
Tested-by: Hudson
Tested-by: Maloo <whamcloud.maloo@gmail.com>
Reviewed-by: Andreas Dilger <andreas.dilger@intel.com>
Reviewed-by: Oleg Drokin <oleg.drokin@intel.com>
